title: RdfConfiguration
description: Configuration for RDF/Knowledge Graph support in OpenMetadata

reasoningLevel.description: Level of reasoning/inference to apply to SPARQL queries

remoteEndpoint.description: SPARQL endpoint URL for remote RDF storage

connectTimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for establishing connections to RDF storage.

requestTimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for individual RDF storage requests.

writeMaxRetries.description: Maximum number of retries for idempotent RDF write requests after the initial attempt.

writeRetryInitialBackoffMs.description: Initial backoff in milliseconds before retrying an RDF write request.

writeRetryMaxBackoffMs.description: Maximum backoff in milliseconds between RDF write request retries.

bulkEntityBatchSize.description: Maximum number of entity models written to RDF storage in a single bulk request.

bulkRelationshipSourceBatchSize.description: Maximum number of source entities reconciled in a single RDF relationship bulk request.

inferenceEnabled.description: Enable inference/reasoning on SPARQL queries. When enabled, SPARQL queries will use the inference engine to derive additional triples based on the reasoning level.

defaultInferenceLevel.description: Default reasoning level for SPARQL queries when inference is enabled. CUSTOM provides OpenMetadata-specific inference rules including transitive lineage traversal and inverse relationships.

cacheInferredTriples.description: Cache inferred triples for better query performance (requires more storage)

federation.description: Controls federated SPARQL access (SERVICE clauses) to external endpoints. Federation is disabled by default; SERVICE clauses are rejected unless the target URI is in the allowlist.

enabled.description: Master switch for federated SPARQL. When false, every SERVICE clause is rejected regardless of allowlist contents.

allowedEndpoints.description: External SPARQL endpoint URIs that may appear in SERVICE clauses. Compared verbatim against the URI in the SERVICE clause; trailing slashes matter.
